_schema.purpose: Historical identifier renames. Validator (scripts/check_renames.py, Check 24) greps for old names in non-exempt docs and warns when a stale reference is found. Add a new entry every time a MAgPIE-side rename lands in develop that affects something the AI docs may have already named.

fields.old: old identifier (regex-safe literal; word-boundary anchored by the checker)
fields.new: replacement identifier or 'REMOVED' if the old identifier was deleted without a 1:1 replacement
fields.exempt_paths: list of glob patterns where the old name is allowed (archives, audit logs, commit messages, this file itself)

renames.reason: PR #869 — pm_timber_yield (tDM/ha/yr flux) replaced by im_growing_stock (tDM/ha stock); consumers divide by m_timestep_length_forestry to recover annual flux
PR #866 — Module 21 trade-cost interface split into three components (tariff, margin, feasibility); single vm_cost_trade replaced by all three in q11_cost_reg
R3 audit found this was fabricated — no such scalar ever existed; M56 mechanism is price-driven CDR, not target-driven

DEMOTED, not deleted: fm_multicropping was a real cross-module (fm_) interface parameter consumed by 42_water_demand until commit 2f3f0fb88 removed that use ('removed fm_multicropping factor because of fallow inconsistency', CHANGELOG.md:671). With no cross-module consumer left it became module-local f18_multicropping(t_all,i), declared in 18_residues (e.g. flexreg_apr16/input.gms:11) and used as sum(ct, f18_multicropping(ct,i2)) at flexreg_apr16/equations.gms:17. The fm_ name is therefore STALE, not fabricated — an easy one to recall wrongly, and it reached reference/GAMS_MAgPIE_Patterns.md inside a fenced GAMS example, where the backtick-anchored Check 14/15 identifier regex could not see it. Check 24 greps raw lines, so it can.
